response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior individual contributor with a genuinely relevant developer-tools and testing background — test frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), CI/CD infrastructure, IDE component work at National Instruments, and current AI developer tooling at Google. However, this is a Level II people-management role requiring 2+ years of people management and 3 years of formal technical leadership, and the resume shows no people-management experience at all and no formal tech-lead or roadmap-owning roles. Mentoring and workshops signal teaching aptitude but do not substitute for managing performance, running one-on-ones, or leading organizational change.
strengths: Deep developer-tools and test-infrastructure experience: test frameworks, test runners, CI servers, device orchestration, and IDE components
18+ years of broad engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded, including high-scale low-latency systems
Strong mentoring and knowledge-sharing record (multi-day Selenium course, workshops, refactoring dojos, conference talks)
concerns: No people-management experience; the role minimum requires 2 years managing or leading a team
No formal technical leadership role evidenced (3 years required) — career is consistently senior/staff IC work
Mobile-platform experience is limited and dated (iOS/Apple TV work in 2015-2016) for a role centered on a large mobile ecosystem

key_factors.factor: Developer tools and testing infrastructure depth
Missing formal technical leadership track record
